what_it_does: Implements the Counterpart Studio application shell: a top bar, a single two-depth navigator (a global icon spine plus a project-zone drawer), a main canvas area, and a floating Ask Vi composer bar.
observations: The layout is driven by a Shell component that renders Topbar, NavSpine, ProjectDrawer, a scrim, and a main canvas.
NavSpine deliberately merges global app destinations (Projects, Templates, Files) and project lifecycle zones on one vertical rail separated by a divider.
ProjectDrawer is described as a continuation of the rail rather than a separate panel, using the same icon-holder and gold-active treatment.
A wrap() helper ensures navigation callbacks first close the off-canvas mobile nav.
The project switcher is a popover inside the drawer with click-outside and Escape handling.
The topbar shows a breadcrumb that switches between an overview crumb and a project/sequence crumb with a Live sequence chip.
React hooks are aliased (useStateShell, useEffectShell, etc.) and the components are exposed on window (Shell, AskBar).
The file relies on window.MS_DATA for zones, pages, projects, and phase metadata.
standouts: The unified NavIcon component is reused for both global destinations and project zones, with a hinge class that visually ties the active zone to the drawer.
The wrap() higher-order helper bundles mobile-nav dismissal with every navigation action.
The project switcher is embedded directly in the drawer header rather than in the topbar.
The brand button contains an inline-styled Vi logo with a radial gradient and an embedded lowercase i.
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props.
Aliased React hooks to avoid naming collisions across concatenated files.
BEM-ish CSS class names and data attributes used as comment anchors.
Global window objects for shared components and data (Shell, AskBar, MS_DATA).
Event listeners registered in useEffect with cleanup functions.
Conditional rendering via ternary expressions for overview vs studio topbar crumbs.
concerns: The brand button uses a large inline style object, mixing presentation with markup.
The nav-scrim has tabIndex={-1}, so it is not keyboard-focusable even though it closes the mobile nav.
Composer is referenced by AskBar but not imported or defined in this file, relying on a global.
All zone/page/project data comes from window.MS_DATA with no local fallback or validation.
The account NavIcon marks active based on activeZone === 'account', which may never match a real zone id.
notes: A single-file React shell that tries to collapse global navigation and project lifecycle zones into one shared rail, with a drawer that is styled to feel like the rail extended.
questions: Where is Composer defined or imported for AskBar?
What is the exact shape of window.MS_DATA (zones, zonePages, projects, getPhase)?
How is navStyle='unfold' interpreted by CSS or parent logic?
What is the sequence object shape and how is it kept in sync with the active page?
Is there focus trapping or focus restoration for the off-canvas mobile nav?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file builds the unified topbar, single spine/drawer navigator, and lifecycle-stage navigation described in the header comments.
intention_signals: The file is trying to reduce navigation fragmentation by placing global and project-level destinations on a single rail, make the zone drawer feel like a natural extension of that rail, and keep project switching and assistant access within one gesture.
novelty: The 'hinge' concept on NavIcon that visually connects the active zone to the drawer, and the deliberate 'one rail' fusion of global app destinations with project lifecycle zones.
contribution: Provides the application's top-level layout and navigation scaffolding: a reusable Shell with topbar, unified spine/drawer navigator, project switcher, and an Ask Vi composer entry point.
summary_for_embedding: React shell component for Counterpart Studio with a topbar, unified global/project zone navigator spine, zone page drawer, project switcher, and floating assistant composer.
intention_for_embedding: To give the application a single, coherent two-depth navigation surface that blends global destinations with project lifecycle stages and keeps the assistant accessible.

symbols.does: Closes the off-canvas navigation by setting navOpen to false.
symbols.description: A small state updater defined in Shell and consumed by wrap(); it ensures the mobile navigation drawer is dismissed before any navigation action proceeds.

symbols.does: Returns a wrapper that closes mobile nav before calling the supplied callback.
symbols.description: A higher-order helper used to decorate navigation handlers so that selecting an item first closes the off-canvas nav and then forwards arguments to the original handler if present.

symbols.does: Renders the application header with menu toggle, brand, breadcrumb, and status chips.
symbols.description: Displays the Counterpart brand, a context-sensitive breadcrumb (Projects vs project/sequence), and a cluster of status/account chips; includes the hamburger button that toggles the mobile navigation.

symbols.does: Renders the single icon rail containing global destinations and, in studio mode, project zones.
symbols.description: Combines app-level tabs (Projects, Templates, Files) and project lifecycle zones on one vertical rail, separated by a divider, with tutorials and account at the bottom; passes selection events through wrapped handlers.

symbols.does: Renders one icon button in the spine, optionally marked active and connected by a hinge.
symbols.description: Shared button component for both global destinations and zones; adds an active class and a connected hinge class that visually links the selected zone to the drawer.

symbols.does: Renders the active zone's page list, zone header, and project switcher popover.
symbols.description: Builds the drawer as a continuation of the spine: shows the current project with a switcher popover, the active zone's label and blurb, and its pages with status indicators; manages popover open/close via click-outside and Escape.

symbols.does: Closes the project switcher popover when clicking outside of it.
symbols.description: A document mousedown handler attached while the switcher is open; it checks popRef containment and sets switching to false when the user clicks outside the project header.

symbols.does: Closes the project switcher popover on Escape key.
symbols.description: A document keydown handler attached while the switcher is open; it dismisses the popover when the user presses the Escape key.
